LAST_DONE:    Phase 8 Batch 2 deferred integration closed. Refactored `packages/jobs/src/processors/er-sync.processor.ts` `syncEvents` to split Prisma `upsert` into `findUnique` + `create`/`update`, so create-vs-update is distinguishable. `enqueueAlert({tenantId, userId:"system", alertRuleId:"", eventId, priority})` called only on the create path, wrapped in try/catch so a queue outage never fails the sync. Updated `packages/jobs/src/__tests__/er-sync.processor.test.ts`: replaced `event.upsert` mock with `findUnique`/`create`/`update`, mocked `../queues/alerts.queue`, replaced single "syncs events" test with 5 new cases — create-path, update-path, enqueue-on-create-only, no-enqueue-on-update, sync-succeeds-on-enqueue-failure. Removed two unnecessary type assertions on toJsonOrNull args (lint flagged after data const extraction). Two-stage review PASS. Lint exit 0, typecheck exit 0, 16/16 tests in 202ms (5 alerts.processor + 11 er-sync).

              IMPORTANT NOTE: The previous STATE.md NEXT had described the integration point as `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/event.ts` `event.create` mutation. That mutation does not exist — the event router only has list/getById/updateState/stats. Events are created via `er-sync.processor.ts syncEvents` from EarthRanger. The actual integration site was the er-sync processor, not the tRPC router. STATE.md was wrong; corrected during this session.
